metadata.title: Build a Quantum Tunneling Simulator with Claude Code + Manim
metadata.color_semantics: Okabe-Ito: TEAL #009E73 = transmission curve, CRIMSON #D55E00 = slope chip/annotations. INK for all text.
metadata.note: Medhavy register. B00=brand intro; B01-B04=tunneling simulation; B05=brand outro. TTS: 'Medhavy' throughout.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'NikBearBrownOpen' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B05: palette=claude but the outro is 'NikBearBrownOutro' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. Nik Bear Brown — Claude Code, Manim, and the physics, taken apart.

The prompt. Claude Code terminal: claude — write a Manim scene, quantum tunneling through a rectangular barrier. V-zero equals one electron-volt, incident energy E equals zero-point-five. Vary barrier width d from zero to two nanometers. Plot log T versus d. Three things in every prompt: the rule, the numbers, what to render.

props.command:
claude "write a Manim scene:
  quantum tunneling, V₀=1 eV, E=0.5 eV
  T ≈ e^(−2κd), κ=√(2m(V₀−E))/ℏ
  sweep d from 0 to 2 nm
  plot log(T) vs d"

beats.narration_text: The CLI writes the file. Before running — read it. Find this line: kappa equals sqrt two m times delta-V over h-bar. That is the decay constant. Check: for V-zero minus E equals zero-point-five electron-volts, kappa comes out one-point-one-five nanometers-inverse. That sets the slope of the log T curve. Thirty seconds. Not optional.

props.code:
# tunneling.py  —  Claude Code output
import numpy as np
hbar = 1.0546e-34  # J·s
m_e  = 9.109e-31   # kg
eV   = 1.602e-19   # J per eV

V0, E = 1.0, 0.5   # eV
dV = (V0 - E) * eV  # 8.01e-20 J

# ← the physics: exponential decay constant
kappa = np.sqrt(2 * m_e * dV) / hbar  # 1.146 nm⁻¹

def T(d_nm):
    d = d_nm * 1e-9
    return np.exp(-2 * kappa * d)
# kappa = 1.146 nm⁻¹  → slope = -2×1.146 = -2.29/nm

beats.narration_text: Run it. Two things to verify. First: at d equals zero, transmission T equals one. The curve starts at the top. No barrier — full transmission. Second: the log T curve is perfectly linear in d. Slope equals negative two times kappa, negative two-point-three per nanometer. Classical physics predicts zero transmission at any positive d. This is the gap. Both checks pass. Ready to iterate.

Iterate: claude — update tunneling dot py — raise V-zero to two electron-volts. Watch kappa increase to one-point-six-two nanometers-inverse and the slope steepen. Each nanometer now kills more current. One number, one check, one re-run. Prompt, read, run, verify, change. The loop is what you carry forward.

props.command:
claude "update tunneling.py:
  → change V₀ from 1 eV to 2 eV
  → show κ increases to 1.62 nm⁻¹
  → overlay new steeper slope on same axes"

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Brutalist and Educational A-I. On YouTube, at Nik Bear Brown. Nik Bear Brown dot com.
